files.detail: Add NormalizedPdfText(str) subclass carrying a page_count attribute; normalize_pdf() returns an instance of it (still fully str-compatible) instead of a plain str. No change to _page_marker, iter_pages, PAGE_MARKER_RE, _PAGE_CLOSE_RE, or the 4.5.15.1 'Trust boundary' docstring paragraphs.
files.risk: low — additive, backward compatible by construction (str subclass)

files.detail: dispatch_pdf reads text.page_count instead of sum(1 for _ in iter_pages(text)); remove now-unused iter_pages import.
files.risk: low — single call site, covered by existing + new tests

files.detail: New test test_dispatch_pdf_page_count_matches_normalize_pdf_no_second_parse, matched by -k page_count: monkeypatches ingestion.normalize_pdf.iter_pages to raise AssertionError if called, then asserts dispatch_pdf's structured_fields['page_count'] is still correct.

explicitly_out_of_scope_confirmed_unaffe: agents/ingestion/test_normalize_pdf.py (not in impacted-modules list; confirmed all its normalize_pdf(...) call sites remain str-compatible, no edits needed)
agents/ingestion/normalize_ticket.py (subtask 4.5.15.4, commit 5be2c91, docstring-only, no file overlap)
agents/ingestion/rawdoc.py (RawDocument dataclass; unaffected, structured_fields is still a plain dict)

4.5.15.1.overlap_with_this_subtask: none — docstring paragraphs vs. this subtask's return-type/logic change to normalize_pdf() and dispatch_pdf(); confirmed via git show --stat, no line-range overlap expected since this subtask edits normalize_pdf()'s function body/signature, not its docstring prose

4.5.15.2.overlap_with_this_subtask: none — test-only, no production file touched, and this subtask does not touch test_normalize_pdf.py

concurrency_check.other_agents_in_shared_worktree: issues #43/#50 (engine/rpc, engine/catalog, engine/btree), #52 (engine/wal) per orchestrator note
concurrency_check.path_overlap_with_this_subtask: none — none of those touch agents/ingestion/
